AIM 25 Project · Silver Generation

Experience is not
a liability.
It's a force.

A strategic initiative to reshape how Auchan Retail Poland thinks about workers aged 50+—turning perceived challenges into competitive advantage.

See our proposal ↓
30%
of Poland's workforce will be 50+ by 2030
The context

Poland's silver challenge

Poland faces one of Europe's fastest-aging workforces. Yet most HR strategies still treat workers over 50 as a short-term cost rather than a long-term investment.

Early retirement incentives, ageist hiring practices, and a lack of upskilling programs are leaving vast reserves of talent untapped—at a time when Poland can least afford it.

Our project reframes this story. The silver generation is not the problem—ignoring them is.

4.2M
Workers aged 50–64 currently active in Poland
+27 yrs
Average experience held by workers in this cohort
2× faster
Knowledge transfer in teams with senior mentors
€18B
Estimated economic cost of early exits from Polish labor market
Our value proposition

A plan built on
3 pillars and 3 horizons.

From demographic challenge to strategic competitive advantage. Our roadmap transforms the Silver Generation from a perceived cost into a performance asset—scalable across all of Auchan Retail.

■

Pillar 1 — Conditions of Work

Adapt working environments and schedules to support experienced employees: ergonomic workstations, flexible hours, part-time transitions, and tailored career review plans.

■

Pillar 2 — Human Development

Recognize expertise and loyalty, build mentoring and coaching roles, foster reverse mentoring with younger employees, and upskill seniors for new responsibilities.

■

Pillar 3 — Process & Organisation

Reinvent jobs through AI and digital tools, simplify processes, build partnerships with schools and universities, and adapt benefits and compensation models.

H1

2026 — Secure the Silver Generation

Maintain and recruit experienced employees, adapt working conditions, recognize expertise and loyalty, and preserve customer relationship know-how.

H2

2027 — Transmission & Development

Transfer knowledge through intergenerational collaboration, create mentoring roles, develop reverse mentoring programs, and build academic partnerships.

H3

2028 — Reinvent the Future

Reinvent jobs through AI and technology, create generational balance, transform leadership models, and position Poland as a future-ready employer.

Our Vision

“Be the favourite employer for the Silver Generation in Poland—and tomorrow, for all generations.”

Our approach

We don't see a weakness.
We see an untapped asset.

01

Reframe the narrative

Challenge outdated assumptions about age and productivity. Silver generation workers bring decades of institutional knowledge, resilience, and mentoring capacity.

02

Build the evidence

Ground our action plan in data. We analyze Polish labor market trends, retention rates, and the measurable ROI of multigenerational teams.

03

Deliver a plan

A concrete, actionable roadmap for HR leaders—covering recruitment, retention, intergenerational collaboration, and knowledge transfer programs.

Resources

Project documents

All research, analysis and deliverables below were produced entirely by our team as part of this project.

Research · Part I
Retail in Poland — Employer Branding Analysis
How the main retailers position themselves as employers, where they recruit, what values they communicate, and how Auchan Polska compares.
Open
Research · Part II
Senior Talent in Retail — What the Sector Does Today
Active programs, real certifications and hard data on how major European retailers already manage Gen X and 50+ workers in 2024–2026.
Open
Survey · February 2026
Voice of the Silver Generation — Employee Survey Results
Real survey conducted in February 2026 among Silver Generation employees at Auchan Retail Poland. Their own words, their reality, their expectations.
View slides
Analysis · Stakeholders
Who Needs to Be on Board — Stakeholder Mapping & Analysis
7 stakeholder groups identified — from senior employees and managers to Executive Leadership and the Works Council. For each group: impacts, expected gains, concerns and support levers.

Who we are

The team behind
the project

Carmen Jorge
Indirect Purchasing Director · Alcampo Spain

Holding an MBA specialising in Big Data, Carmen has been with Auchan for 14 years—from hypermarket sales and management control to Head of Management Control at headquarters, and now Indirect Purchasing Director since June 2024. She brings a strong analytical background and hands-on experience driving organisational change across complex, international environments.

Mahdi Boukennat
Director of Social Affairs · Auchan France

Graduated in Labor Law and Corporate Industrial Relations, Mahdi has spent over 12 years at Auchan, progressing from Labor Law Specialist to HR & Social Affairs Manager, Head of Social Affairs, and now Director of Social Affairs. His expertise spans legal and labor relations, union strategy, collective bargaining, and supporting major business and organizational transformations.

Assietou Dramé
HR Director, Auchan Senegal & Côte d’Ivoire

Across Africa, a company like Auchan can change lives — and that’s what drives me. I’m Assietou Dramé, HR Director for Auchan Senegal and Côte d’Ivoire, leading HR, CSR, and communication. Ten years with Auchan, four in this role, have taught me that business is one of the most powerful levers for development: done right, it lifts employees, customers, and communities alike. That’s the impact I’m proud to shape every day.
